change_to_normalize_hypothesis_before_sp.tactic: change (gsStepWith prof σ m₀ w₀).matching.womenMatch w = some m at hmatch
change_to_normalize_hypothesis_before_sp.trigger: hmatch contains unreduced match/gsStepWith expressions that don't match local set/let definitions
change_to_normalize_hypothesis_before_sp.prerequisite: must have set m₀ := Classical.choose hfree and set w₀ := gsChooseMax prof σ m₀ hm₀.2 before
change_to_normalize_hypothesis_before_sp.next_step: simp only [gsStepWith] at hmatch; split at hmatch

split_at_hmatch_after_simp_only.tactic:
simp only [gsStepWith] at hmatch
split at hmatch
split_at_hmatch_after_simp_only.trigger: after simp only [DefName], a match expression remains that simp [discriminant_eq] cannot reduce
split_at_hmatch_after_simp_only.failure_avoided: simp [hwm] reports 'made no progress' — fundamental limitation of simp on match

rcases_before_subst_anti_reflexivity.tactic:
rcases hsrc with (hw | ⟨rfl, rfl⟩)
· subst hw; ...
· exact le_rfl
rcases_before_subst_anti_reflexivity.trigger: rcases with ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ may unify variables, making hw : w₀ = w₀ reflexive, and subst fails
rcases_before_subst_anti_reflexivity.failure_avoided: subst hw fails with 'reflexive equality' when hw : w₀ = w₀ after prior unification

explicit_mem_iff_arguments.trigger: proposedSet.mem_iff (m', w) gives type mismatch — needs full @ with n, inst, prof, σ
explicit_mem_iff_arguments.failure_avoided: Application type mismatch: (m', w) expected to be PrefProfile

dsimp_before_rw_on_match_constructs.tactic:
dsimp [GSMatching.swapMatch] at hmatch
rw [Function.update_apply] at hmatch
dsimp_before_rw_on_match_constructs.trigger: rw [Function.update_apply] fails when hmatch still contains unreduced swapMatch/matchFree definition
dsimp_before_rw_on_match_constructs.failure_avoided: rw [Function.update_apply] fails — need dsimp first to unfold the structural definition

show_instead_of_unfold_for_noncomputable.trigger: unfold fails or produces unreadable goals on noncomputable def := by ... definitions
show_instead_of_unfold_for_noncomputable.alternative: use show to explicitly state the type, or simp only [def_name]

patterns.name: change_normalize
patterns.when: hypothesis contains raw Classical.choose/gsChooseMax that don't match local definitions
patterns.tactic: change (normalized_expr) at hmatch

examples.tactic_used: change (gsStepWith prof σ m₀ w₀).matching.womenMatch w = some m at hmatch
examples.key_insight: After set m₀/set w₀, hmatch still contains raw gsStep/gsChooseMax. change normalizes to the unfolded form

patterns.name: split_at_for_match
patterns.when: simp only [Def] leaves irreducible match expression in hypothesis
patterns.tactic: split at hmatch

examples.tactic_used: simp only [gsStepWith] at hmatch; split at hmatch
examples.key_insight: gsStepWith has match on womenMatch w. split creates none/some branches

examples.tactic_used: unfold gsStepWith; split
examples.key_insight: After unfold of match-based def, split creates the branch structure

patterns.name: rcases_before_subst
patterns.when: need both rcases and subst on potentially reflexive equality
patterns.tactic: rcases h with (... | ⟨rfl, rfl⟩); · subst hw; ...; · exact le_rfl
not: subst hw; rcases h — reflexive equality blocks subst

examples.tactic_used: rcases hsrc with (hw' | ⟨rfl, rfl⟩)
examples.key_insight: When source is Or of (old_proposed) and (m=m₀ ∧ w=w₀), the rfl branch unifies variables

patterns.name: explicit_@_args
patterns.when: lemma has implicit args that Lean cannot infer in context
patterns.tactic: @LemmaName n _ prof σ (m', w)

examples.tactic_used: (@proposedSet.mem_iff n _ prof (gsStepWith prof σ m₀ w₀) (m', w)).mpr hprop
examples.key_insight: proposedSet.mem_iff needs n, NeZero n instance, prof, σ as implicit args before the pair

patterns.name: consistency_chain_via_iff
patterns.when: GSConsistent hypothesis h : GSConsistent μ provides iff for each pair
patterns.tactic:
have := (h m w).mp heq   -- forward
have := (h m w).mpr heq  -- backward
have := (h m w).symm     -- reversed iff

examples.tactic_used: have hmOld : σ.matching.menMatch mOld = some w := (h mOld w).mpr hwm
examples.key_insight: Consistency gives iff menMatch↔womenMatch. Use .mp for men→women, .mpr for women→men

examples.tactic_used: have : μ.womenMatch w' ≠ some m := by intro heq; have := (h m w').mpr heq; simp_all
examples.key_insight: To prove inequality, assume equality then derive contradiction via consistency chain

patterns.name: function_update_split_ifs
patterns.when: goal or hypothesis contains Function.update (from matchFree/swapMatch)
patterns.tactic:
simp only [GSMatching.matchFree, Function.update_apply]
split_ifs
after: exact h m' w' for default case

examples.tactic_used: simp only [GSMatching.matchFree, Function.update_apply]; split_ifs
examples.key_insight: update_apply creates if-then-else. split_ifs creates 4 branches: m'=m/w'=w/both/neither

examples.tactic_used: simp only [GSMatching.swapMatch, Function.update_apply]; split_ifs with h_mOld h_ww h_m h_nw h_nm
examples.key_insight: Nested updates create nested ifs. Name them for clarity. 6 branches for double update

patterns.name: invariant_induction_with_by_cases
patterns.when: proving invariant holds for all k steps of gsRunSteps
patterns.tactic:
induction k with
| zero => exact initial_prof
| succ k' ih =>
  simp only [gsRunSteps]
  by_cases h : ∃ m, gsIsFree prof (gsRunSteps prof k') m
  · exact step prof ih h
  · have hid : gsStep prof (gsRunSteps prof k') = gsRunSteps prof k' := by
      unfold gsStep; simp [h]
    rw [hid]; exact ih

examples.tactic_used: induction k with zero/succ, by_cases on free man existence
examples.key_insight: Step case: either a free man exists (use step lemma) or not (gsStep is identity). Identity proved via unfold+simp

examples.tactic_used: Same induction+by_cases pattern
examples.key_insight: Universal pattern for runSteps: initial + step + identity when terminated

patterns.name: Classical.choose_spec_for_existentials
patterns.when: hypothesis is ∃ x, P x and need witness and property separately
patterns.tactic:
let m := Classical.choose hfree
have hm : gsIsFree prof σ m := Classical.choose_spec hfree

examples.tactic_used: let m := Classical.choose hfree; have hm := Classical.choose_spec hfree
examples.key_insight: choose gives witness, choose_spec gives the property. Always use together

patterns.name: bijective_injectivity_for_eq
patterns.when: need to prove w = Classical.choose ⋯ from Bijective (prof.menPref m) and heq : prof.menPref m w = prof.menPref m (Classical.choose ⋯)
patterns.tactic:
have hbij : Bijective (prof.menPref m) := prof.menPref_bijective m
have hinj : (prof.menPref m).Injective := hbij.injective
exact hinj heq

examples.tactic_used: have := prof.menPref_bijective m; exact this.injective heq
examples.key_insight: menPref_bijective gives Bijective, .injective gives Injective, then apply to equality hypothesis. For PrefProfile, menPref m is a bijection Fin n → Fin n

patterns.name: finset_maximal_choose_spec_negation
patterns.when: need to show prof.menPref m (Classical.choose ⋯) < prof.menPref m w from maximality of Classical.choose in Finset
patterns.tactic:
obtain ⟨hmem, hmax⟩ := Classical.choose_spec (Finset.exists_maximal h)
have : ¬ gsMenPrefLE prof m w (Classical.choose ⋯) := hmax w hw
unfold gsMenPrefLE at this
push_neg at this
exact this.resolve_left (fun heq => ...)

examples.tactic_used: obtain ⟨hmem, hmax⟩ := Classical.choose_spec ...; have := hmax w hw
examples.key_insight: exists_maximal gives ⟨membership, maximality⟩. Maximality says ¬(w ≤ choose), i.e. ¬(w = choose ∨ menPref choose < menPref w). Negation gives w ≠ choose ∧ ¬(menPref choose < menPref w). Combined with hgt (menPref w < menPref choose), contradiction on ¬(menPref choose < menPref w)

patterns.name: custom_LE_instance_for_finite
patterns.when: need LE instance on Fin n for gsMenPrefLE or similar custom preorder
patterns.tactic:
letI : LE (Fin n) := ⟨gsMenPrefLE prof m⟩
haveI : IsTrans (Fin n) (· ≤ ·) :=
  ⟨fun a b c hab hbc => by
    cases hab with
    | inl hab => subst hab; exact hbc
    | inr hab =>
      cases hbc with
      | inl hbc => subst hbc; exact Or.inr hab
      | inr hbc => exact Or.inr (lt_trans hbc hab)⟩

examples.tactic_used: letI : LE (Fin n) := ⟨gsMenPrefLE prof m⟩; haveI : IsTrans ...
examples.key_insight: Mathlib's Finset.exists_maximal needs LE + IsTrans. Build locally via letI/haveI

patterns.name: maximal_trichotomy_proof_strategy
patterns.when: need to prove ∀ y ∈ s, y ≤ choose where choose comes from Classical.choose (Finset.exists_maximal h) and LE is a custom preorder on Fin n backed by Nat comparison (e.g. gsMenPrefLE)
patterns.tactic:
obtain ⟨-, hmax⟩ := Classical.choose_spec (Finset.exists_maximal h)
have htri := Nat.lt_trichotomy (prof.menPref m w) (prof.menPref m (Classical.choose ⋯))
rcases htri with (hlt | heq | hgt)
· -- choose ≤ w: apply hmax to get w ≤ choose
  right; exact hgt
· -- equal prefs: injectivity gives w = choose
  left; exact (prof.menPref_bijective m).injective heq
· -- w preferred over choose: direct
  right; exact hgt

examples.tactic_used: trichotomy on menPref values + Maximal conditional + Bijective.injective for eq case
examples.key_insight: Three-part strategy: (1) Nat.lt_trichotomy to case-split on the underlying Nat comparison. (2) Case choose<w: need choose≤w first, then hmax hw gives w≤choose. (3) Case equal: Bijective.injective turns pref equality into Fin equality → Or.inl. (4) Case choose>w: Or.inr directly. CRITICAL: hmax from Maximal is CONDITIONAL — hmax hw has type (choose ≤ w → w ≤ choose), NOT w ≤ choose directly.

common_mistakes: Applying hmax w hw directly without establishing choose ≤ w first — type mismatch
Using unfold on gsChooseMax (noncomputable def with by body) — use show instead
Forgetting that Maximal 2nd field is ∀ y ∈ s, choose ≤ y → y ≤ choose (conditional), NOT ∀ y ∈ s, y ≤ choose

patterns.name: injection_for_option_equality
patterns.when: hypothesis is some x = some y or none = none after Function.update reduction
patterns.tactic:
injection hmatch with hw; subst hw
-- or
injection hmatch with hmi; subst hmi

examples.tactic_used: injection hmatch with hw; right; exact ⟨‹m' = m›, hw.symm⟩
examples.key_insight: After Function.update_apply + split_ifs, equality some w = some w' appears. injection extracts w = w'

patterns.name: proposedSet_stepWith_insert_pattern
patterns.when: reasoning about whether a pair was proposed before or after gsStepWith
patterns.tactic:
have hmem := (@proposedSet.mem_iff n _ prof (gsStepWith prof σ m₀ w₀) (m', w)).mpr hprop
rw [proposedSet.stepWith_insert prof σ m₀ w₀] at hmem
simp only [Finset.mem_insert, Prod.mk.injEq, proposedSet.mem_iff] at hmem
cases hmem with | inl h => right; exact h | inr h => left; exact h

examples.tactic_used: Full pattern: mem_iff.mpr → stepWith_insert → simp → cases
examples.key_insight: stepWith_insert says proposedSet after step = insert (m₀,w₀) old. So membership is (m',w)=(m₀,w₀) ∨ old_member

patterns.name: by_contra_obtain_contradiction
patterns.when: negating a complex existential or disjunction to derive impossibility
patterns.tactic:
by_contra hnot
obtain ⟨a, ha, c, hc, hab, hcb, hac, hfab, hfbc⟩ := not_extremal ... hnot
-- derive cycle/contradiction from obtained witnesses

examples.tactic_used: by_contra hnot; obtain ⟨a, ha, c, hc, ...⟩ := not_extremal ... hnot
examples.key_insight: Arrow's extremal lemma: if b not extremal, get a,c with P a b and P b c, then use IIA to derive contradiction via Pareto

patterns.name: suffices_for_goal_restructuring
patterns.when: goal can be reduced to a stronger intermediate claim that implies original
patterns.tactic:
suffices h : ∀ D, ... → ∃ n, ... by
  -- prove main goal from h
  ...
  -- prove h (often by induction)

examples.tactic_used: suffices h : ∀ D prof, ... → ∃ n, is_pivotal ... by exact h Finset.univ all_bot ...
examples.key_insight: Separate existence of pivot (from initial worst profile) from induction on D.card

patterns.name: strong_induction_on_nat
patterns.when: induction on Finset cardinality or Nat with decreasing argument
patterns.tactic:
induction k using Nat.strongRecOn with
| ind m IH =>
  -- use IH for strictly smaller values
  exact IH D''.card hD''lt_m D'' prof'' ...

examples.tactic_used: induction k using Nat.strongRecOn with | ind m IH =>
examples.key_insight: Well-founded induction on D.card. Each step picks i from D, flips to top, recurses on D\{i}

patterns.name: constructive_counterexample_profile
patterns.when: proving impossibility by constructing specific profile that yields contradiction
patterns.tactic:
let base : PrefOrder σ := ⟨fun _ _ => True, fun _ => trivial, fun _ _ => Or.inl trivial, fun _ _ _ _ _ => trivial⟩
let prof : Profile ι σ := fun k =>
  if k = i then maketop_pref base a
  else if k = j then maketop_pref base b
  else maketop_pref base a
use prof
-- then prove cycle P a b ∧ P b d ∧ P d a

examples.tactic_used: Multiple profile constructions with maketop_pref/makebot_pref for each sub-case
examples.key_insight: Sen's proof: construct profile where decisive rights + Pareto create preference cycle. 5+ sub-cases depending on pair overlap

examples.tactic_used: let prof : Profile ι σ := fun i => if i = prude then maketop_pref (makebot_pref base lr) np else ...
examples.key_insight: Book paradox: Prude makes np top/lr bottom, Lewd makes lr top/pr bottom, creating cycle

patterns.name: IIA_same_order_transfer
patterns.when: transferring strict preference between profiles via Independence of Irrelevant Alternatives
patterns.tactic:
have hIIA : same_order (f prof).rel (f prof').rel a b a b :=
  (same_order_iff_same_order' (f prof).total (f prof').total).2 <|
    hind prof prof' a b ha hb fun j => by ...
have Rab' : (f prof').rel a b := hIIA.1.1.1 hfab

examples.tactic_used: same_order_iff_same_order' bridge + hind application + field extraction
examples.key_insight: IIA gives same_order which is an iff on P. Extract forward/backward via .1.1.1/.2.1.1 etc

patterns.name: unfold_ring_for_arithmetic
patterns.when: proving arithmetic identities on margin functions or integer sums
patterns.tactic:
unfold margin
ring

examples.tactic_used: unfold margin; ring
examples.key_insight: margin x y - margin y x is ring-solvable after unfolding definition

patterns.name: linarith_for_linear_arithmetic
patterns.when: combining inequalities from margin_pos and margin_antisymm
patterns.tactic:
have := margin_antisymm prof x y
linarith

examples.tactic_used: have := margin_antisymm prof x y; linarith
examples.key_insight: Two contradictory margin_pos facts (x beats y AND y beats x) → linarith closes from antisymmetry

examples.tactic_used: Same pattern: margin_antisymm + linarith
examples.key_insight: Condorcet winner has margin > 0, loser has margin < 0, antisymmetry gives contradiction

patterns.name: omega_for_nat_arithmetic
patterns.when: proving Nat inequalities or equalities involving card, subtraction, or bounds
patterns.tactic: omega

examples.tactic_used: injection hmold with heq; subst heq; exact by omega
examples.key_insight: When womenPref equal, goal reduces to le_refl or le_of_lt, omega closes automatically

patterns.name: calc_for_telemetry_bounds
patterns.when: proving upper bound on Finset cardinality via chain of inequalities
patterns.tactic:
calc expr₁ ≤ expr₂ := Finset.card_le_card (Finset.filter_subset _ _)
  _ = target := by simp only [Finset.card_univ, Fintype.card_prod, Fintype.card_fin]

examples.tactic_used: calc with card_le_card + simp card_univ/card_prod
examples.key_insight: Proposed set ⊆ univ, so card ≤ card univ = n*n

patterns.name: simp_only_then_split_ifs
patterns.when: goal has Function.update or if-then-else from structural definitions
patterns.tactic:
simp only [DefName, Function.update_apply] at hmatch ⊢
split_ifs

examples.tactic_used: simp only [GSMatching.matchFree, Function.update_apply]; split_ifs
examples.key_insight: Do NOT use simp [] (too aggressive, may break structure). Use simp only with specific lemmas

patterns.name: simp_all_for_local_context
patterns.when: local context has contradictions that simp alone doesn't find
patterns.tactic:
simp_all
-- or
simp_all [ne_comm]

examples.tactic_used: simp_all [ne_comm]
examples.key_insight: simp_all uses ALL hypotheses. ne_comm helps when inequality is in wrong direction

patterns.name: ext_for_finset_extensionality
patterns.when: proving two Finsets equal by showing membership equivalence
patterns.tactic:
ext i
simp [Finset.mem_filter]
-- or
ext ⟨m', w'⟩
simp only [mem_iff, mem_insert, Prod.mk.injEq]

examples.tactic_used: ext ⟨m', w'⟩; simp only [mem_iff, mem_insert, Prod.mk.injEq]
examples.key_insight: Extensionality for Finset (Fin n × Fin n): decompose pair, simplify with explicit membership lemmas

patterns.name: classical_dec_for_filter
patterns.when: Finset.filter needs Decidable instance that isn't automatically available
patterns.tactic:
classical
-- then simp/filter work

examples.tactic_used: classical; simp [proposedCount, proposedSet, gsInitial]
examples.key_insight: Proposed set uses Decidable on proposed (which is Prop, not Decidable). classical provides it

patterns.name: P_trans_chain_for_strict_preference
patterns.when: combining strict preferences P a b and P b c to derive cycle contradiction
patterns.tactic:
have hPad := P_trans (f prof).trans hPa_b hPb_d
exact hPad.2 hPd_a.1

examples.tactic_used: P_trans (f prof).trans hPa_b hPb_d
examples.key_insight: P_trans gives P a d from P a b and P b c. Then .2 gives ¬P d a, contradicting Pareto result

patterns.name: split_ifs_for_equality_case_analysis
patterns.when: constructive proof with if-then-else in profile definition
patterns.tactic:
unfold prof; split_ifs with hki hkj
· -- case k = i
· -- case k = j
· -- other case

examples.tactic_used: unfold prof; split_ifs with hki hkj
examples.key_insight: Profile is fun k => if k=i then ... else if k=j then ... else ... split_ifs creates 3 cases

patterns.name: simp_with_explicit_defs_for_relation_unfolding
patterns.when: proving strict preference P in constructed profile with maketop/makebot
patterns.tactic: simp [P, maketop_pref, maketop_rel, makebot_pref, makebot_rel, hab, had, hbd]

examples.tactic_used: simp [P, maketop_pref, maketop_rel, makebot_pref, makebot_rel, hab, had, hbd]
examples.key_insight: P and maketop/makebot are defined via simp lemmas. Include inequality hypotheses to close branches

patterns.name: push_neg_for_negated_universal
patterns.when: ¬∀ x, P x needs to become ∃ x, ¬P x
patterns.tactic: push_neg at h

examples.tactic_used: push_neg at h; exact h hb
examples.key_insight: ¬is_strictly_worst ↔ ∃ a ∈ X, a ≠ b ∧ ¬P R a b. push_neg converts ∀→∃ automatically

patterns.name: contradiction_for_disjoint_cases
patterns.when: local context has both P a b and ¬P a b (or equivalent)
patterns.tactic:
exact hPa_b.2 hPb_a.1
-- or
contradiction

examples.tactic_used: exact hPpq.2 hPqp.1
examples.key_insight: P R p q gives (R p q, ¬R q p). P R q p gives (R q p, ¬R p q). Second components contradict first components

patterns.name: field_simp_linarith_for_division
patterns.when: proving equalities involving division by positive reals
patterns.tactic:
field_simp
linarith

examples.tactic_used: field_simp; linarith
examples.key_insight: When goal is φ G i = 1/T.card and context has T.card * φ G i = 1 with T.card ≠ 0

patterns.name: Finset.sum_eq_zero_for_null_sum
patterns.when: proving a sum is zero by showing each term is zero
patterns.tactic:
apply Finset.sum_eq_zero
intro S hS
simp only [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and] at hS
-- prove each term = 0

examples.tactic_used: apply Finset.sum_eq_zero; intro S hS; ... simp [hmc]
examples.key_insight: Null player has zero marginal contribution everywhere, so each term in sum is 0

patterns.name: set_vs_let_for_unfolding
patterns.when: need to use definitional equality in hypotheses without introducing new names
patterns.tactic:
set m₀ := Classical.choose hfree
set w₀ := gsChooseMax prof σ m₀ hm₀.2
not: let m₀ := ... — let doesn't create reducible equality for change/simp

examples.tactic_used: set m₀ := Classical.choose hfree; set w₀ := gsChooseMax prof σ m₀ hm₀.2
examples.key_insight: set creates both the local definition AND a hypothesis m₀ = Classical.choose hfree usable by change/simp

patterns.name: have_for_consistency_negation
patterns.when: proving μ.womenMatch w' ≠ some m by assuming equality and using consistency
patterns.tactic:
have : μ.womenMatch w' ≠ some m := by
  intro heq
  have := (h m w').mpr heq
  simp_all

examples.tactic_used: have : μ.womenMatch w' ≠ some m := by intro heq; have := (h m w').mpr heq; simp_all
examples.key_insight: Assume womenMatch = some m, then consistency gives menMatch = some w', contradicting hm (menMatch = none)

patterns.name: by_cases_for_equality_decisions
patterns.when: need to split on whether two terms are equal (in Fin context or option values)
patterns.tactic:
by_cases hw : w = w₀
· rw [if_pos hw] at hmatch; ...
· rw [if_neg hw] at hmatch; ...

examples.tactic_used: by_cases hw : w = w₀; · rw [if_pos hw]; · rw [if_neg hw]
examples.key_insight: After Function.update_apply, need to resolve if w = w₀. by_cases creates both branches explicitly

examples.tactic_used: by_cases hw : w = w₀
examples.key_insight: Same pattern for matchFree branch: w=w₀ means new match, w≠w₀ means old match preserved

patterns.name: exfalso_for_deriving_false
patterns.when: goal is a non-False proposition but local context contains contradictions
patterns.tactic:
exfalso
-- now goal is False
exact h hw

examples.tactic_used: by_contra hn; ... exact lt_irrefl _ hlt
examples.key_insight: by_contra + obtain + derive reflexive inequality. Also: exfalso before applying contradictory hypotheses

patterns.name: subst_chain_for_equality
patterns.when: have equality hypothesis and need to propagate substitution
patterns.tactic:
subst hbc
-- or
subst this; rfl

examples.tactic_used: subst hbc (multiple sub-cases)
examples.key_insight: When by_cases gives b=c, subst propagates into all occurrences, potentially simplifying profile construction

examples.tactic_used: have : k = 0 := Nat.eq_zero_of_le_zero hkj; subst this; rfl
examples.key_insight: Base case: k=0+j=0, subst collapses to rfl

patterns.name: dif_pos_dif_neg_for_dependent_if
patterns.when: goal has dite (dependent if-then-else) from noncomputable definitions
patterns.tactic:
rw [dif_pos hT]  -- select True branch
-- or
rw [dif_neg hF]  -- select False branch

patterns.name: card_insert_of_notMem
patterns.when: proving cardinality after inserting element not in set
patterns.tactic: exact card_insert_of_notMem hmem

examples.tactic_used: exact card_insert_of_notMem hmem
examples.key_insight: insert (m,w) increases card by 1 when (m,w) not already in proposedSet

patterns.name: simp_only_gsStep_dif_pos
patterns.when: unfolding gsStep when a free man exists (dif_pos case)
patterns.tactic:
unfold gsStep
rw [dif_pos hfree]

examples.tactic_used: unfold gsStep; rw [dif_pos hfree]
examples.key_insight: gsStep = if hfree then stepWith else identity. dif_pos selects stepWith branch

patterns.name: gsStep_identity_when_terminated
patterns.when: proving gsStep is identity when no free man exists
patterns.tactic:
unfold gsStep gsTerminated at *
split
· contradiction
· rfl

examples.tactic_used: unfold gsStep gsTerminated at *; split; · contradiction; · rfl
examples.key_insight: gsStep = if ∃ free then ... else σ. When terminated (∀ ¬free), the else branch is σ, so rfl

patterns.name: norm_cast_for_nat_real_conversion
patterns.when: mixing Nat and Real/Int arithmetic (card ↦ ℝ sums)
patterns.tactic:
norm_cast
-- or
exact mod_cast ...

patterns.name: plan_multi_GS_CHOOSEMAX_MAXIMAL_zai
patterns.when: Finset.exists_maximal gives conditional maximality (∀ y ∈ s,
patterns.after:
1. Unfold gsChooseMax and set up LE + IsTrans instances (same as gsChooseMax_mem pattern)
  2. Obtain the spec from Classical.choose_spec (Finset.exists_maximal h) — note maximality is CONDITIONAL:

failed_approaches.what: Classical.choose_spec on Finset.exists_maximal gives CONDITIONAL maximality
failed_approaches.why: Finset.exists_maximal returns: ∃ x ∈ s, ∀ y ∈ s, x ≤ y → y ≤ x (NOT ∀ y ∈ s, y ≤ x). The second component of choose_spec has type: ∀ ⦃y⦄, y ∈ s → choose ≤ y → y ≤ choose. Direct application hmax w hw fails because hmax expects choose ≤ w as its second argument, not membership.
failed_approaches.lesson: To prove ∀ y ∈ s, y ≤ choose: first establish choose ≤ y (by trichotomy of the underlying comparison), then apply hmax to get y ≤ choose. For gsMenPrefLE specifically: use Nat.lt_trichotomy on menPref values to case-split, then combine with maximality.
failed_approaches.correct_approach: unfold gsChooseMax; obtain ⟨-, hmax⟩ := Classical.choose_spec (Finset.exists_maximal h); then trichotomy on menPref w vs menPref choose: if choose < w → done (goal second disjunct); if choose > w → choose ≤ w via gsMenPrefLE → hmax gives w ≤ choose (done); if equal → need strict prefs or w = choose

failed_approaches.what: unfold on noncomputable def with by body
failed_approaches.why: unfold produces the full tactic proof body as the definition, creating unreadable goals
failed_approaches.lesson: Use show to explicitly state the goal type, or simp only [def_name] instead

failed_approaches.why: simp cannot reduce match expressions even with discriminant equality hypothesis
failed_approaches.lesson: After simp only [Def], use split at h to create branches from the match

failed_approaches.what: subst on reflexive equality after rcases ⟨rfl, rfl⟩
failed_approaches.why: rcases with ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ may unify variables making a subsequent equality hw : w = w₀ become hw : w₀ = w₀
failed_approaches.lesson: Use rcases to split first, handle rfl branch with exact le_rfl, then subst in non-rfl branch

failed_approaches.what: Implicit lemma application without @ on parameterized types
failed_approaches.why: Lean cannot infer implicit args (n, NeZero n, prof, σ) from a single pair argument
failed_approaches.lesson: Always use @LemmaName n _ prof σ (m', w) when lemma has type-level parameters

failed_approaches.what: rw [Function.update_apply] before dsimp on structural definitions
failed_approaches.why: rw fails when hypothesis still contains unreduced swapMatch/matchFree wrapper
failed_approaches.lesson: dsimp [GSMatching.swapMatch] first to unfold the structure, then rw [Function.update_apply]

failed_approaches.why: aesop cannot synthesize constructive witnesses for existentials
omega only handles linear arithmetic over Nat/Int, not preference orderings
failed_approaches.lesson: For preference relations, use transitivity lemmas (P_trans, le_trans) and contradiction explicitly

failed_approaches.what: simp without only on structural definitions
failed_approaches.why: simp [] may unfold too aggressively, breaking the structure needed for split_ifs
failed_approaches.lesson: Always use simp only [specific_lemma] to control unfolding. Then split_ifs for the branches

failed_approaches.why: Finset.image uses Quot.lift internally, which is not inductive — obtain/rcases fail
failed_approaches.lesson: Use simpa [hm] or exact for membership goals involving Finset.image
examples_affected: Any proof involving image of Finset

failed_approaches.why: Missing any of fwd_mem, inv_mem, left_inv, right_inv, hfg causes type error
failed_approaches.lesson: Prepare all 5 proofs before calling Finset.sum_bij'. Each obligation is a separate have

failed_approaches.why: womenBestState says: womenMatch w = some m → proposed m' w → m is best. The contrapositive needs proposed m' w → womenMatch w ≠ none, but womenBestState alone doesn't give this — it only talks about women who ARE matched. Need womenProposedImpliesMatched or GSConsistent as additional hypothesis.
failed_approaches.lesson: womenUnproposed needs either (1) reformulate with womenProposedImpliesMatched hypothesis, or (2) prove womenBestState → womenProposedImpliesMatched as auxiliary, or (3) add both hypotheses. The lemma's current formulation is underconstrained.

failed_approaches.what_failed:
unfold gsChooseMax gsMenPrefLE
  letI : LE (Fin n) := ⟨gsMenPrefLE prof m⟩
  haveI : IsTrans (Fin n) (· ≤ ·) :=
    ⟨fun a b c hab hbc => by
      cas

Finset.exists_maximal.signature: Finset.exists_maximal {α : Type*} [LE α] [IsTrans α (· ≤ ·)] (s : Finset α) (h : s.Nonempty) : ∃ i, Maximal (· ∈ s) i
notes: Maximal i means ∀ j ∈ s, i ≤ j → i = j (no strictly greater element in s)
NOT the maximum element — maximal means local maximum via OrderDual
Use Classical.choose_spec to extract both membership and maximality
Requires LE instance and IsTrans instance — build locally with letI/haveI if needed
The IsTrans proof for gsMenPrefLE requires case analysis on Or.inl (equality) / Or.inr (less-than)

usage_example.context: gsChooseMax uses exists_maximal to pick a candidate that no unproposed candidate is strictly preferred to

Function.update_apply.signature: Function.update_apply [DecidableEq α] (f : α → β) (a : α) (b : β) (a' : α) : update f a b a' = if a' = a then b else f a'
notes: Central lemma for reasoning about partial matchings
Creates if-then-else that needs split_ifs
MUST dsimp structural wrappers (matchFree/swapMatch) before using this

Finset.card_le_card.signature: Finset.card_le_card {α : Type*} (h : s₁ ⊆ s₂) : s₁.card ≤ s₂.card
notes: Useful for proving upper bounds on filtered sets

Finset.card_insert_of_notMem.signature: Finset.card_insert_of_notMem {a : α} (h : a ∉ s) : (insert a s).card = s.card + 1
notes: Key for proving proposal count increment by 1

Finset.sum_bij'.signature: Finset.sum_bij' {α β M : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] (f : α → β) (g : β → α) (hfg : ∀ b ∈ t, f (g b) = b) ... : ∑ a ∈ s, h a = ∑ b ∈ t, h (f (g b))
notes: 5 obligations: fwd_mem, inv_mem, left_inv, right_inv, hfg
Used in Shapley uniqueness proof for bijective reindexing of coalition sums
Prepare each obligation as a separate have before the main call

Finset.sum_eq_single.signature: Finset.sum_eq_single {α : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] (s : Finset α) (a : α) (h_mem : a ∈ s) (h_zero : ∀ b ∈ s, b ≠ a → f b = 0) : ∑ b ∈ s, f b = f a
notes: Useful when only one element in a sum contributes a nonzero value

Finset.sum_eq_zero.signature: Finset.sum_eq_zero {α : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] (h : ∀ a ∈ s, f a = 0) : ∑ a ∈ s, f a = 0
notes: Used for null player axiom: all marginal contributions are zero

same_order_iff_same_order'.signature: same_order_iff_same_order' {R R' : σ → σ → Prop} (hR : Total R) (hR' : Total R') : same_order R R' a b c d ↔ same_order' R R' a b c d
notes: Bridge between same_order (4-arg) and same_order' (4-arg with iff-based P equivalence)
Critical for Arrow IIA proofs: extract via .2 then apply hind

nP_of_reverseP.signature: nP_of_reverseP {R : σ → σ → Prop} {a b : σ} (h : P R a b) : ¬P R b a
notes: Asymmetry of strict preference P. Use to derive ¬P from P in opposite direction

lt_asymm.signature: lt_asymm {a b : α} (h : a < b) : ¬b < a
notes: Used in gsChooseMax_maximal proof to derive contradiction from maximality violation

Nat.strongRecOn.signature: Nat.strongRecOn {motive : Nat → Sort*} (n : Nat) (h : ∀ n, (∀ m < n, motive m) → motive n) : motive n
notes: Well-founded induction on Nat. Use for decreasing Finset.card arguments
The induction hypothesis applies to ALL m < n, not just n-1

Finset.induction.signature: Finset.induction {p : Finset α → Prop} (s : Finset α) (h_empty : p ∅) (h_insert : ∀ a s, ¬a ∈ s → p s → p (insert a s)) : p s
notes: Induction on Finset structure. Used in Shapley for coalition-based proofs
Provides Decidable for any Prop via classical logic
Needed when Finset.filter requires Decidable instance for non-decidable predicates
Use classical at start of proof or haveI : ... := Classical.dec _ for specific instances

makeabove_rel_noteq_P.signature: makeabove_rel_noteq_P {r : σ → σ → Prop} {a b c d : σ} (hac : a ≠ c) (hdb : d ≠ b) : same_order' r (makeabove_rel r a b) d c d c
notes: Key IIA lemma: makeabove preserves strict preference for pairs not involving the raised element
Returns iff (same_order'), not just implication
Use .symm on both components to get forward direction

Finset.sum_add_sum_compl.signature: Finset.sum_add_sum_compl {α M : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α] (s : Finset α) (f : α → M) : ∑ a ∈ s, f a + ∑ a ∈ sᶜ, f a = ∑ a, f a
notes: Partition sum into set and complement. Used in Shapley unanimity proof

GSState.lean.gsChooseMax_maximal: No unproposed candidate preferred over chosen one

Lemmas.lean.proposedCount.runSteps_eq_of_not_termina: Count equals k when not terminated
Lemmas.lean.proposedCount.step_of_free: Step for free man increases count
Lemmas.lean.gsRunSteps_eq_of_terminated: All subsequent steps identity after termination

Arrow.lean.not_extremal: Non-extremal implies sandwich: ∃ a,c with R a b ∧ R b c
Arrow.lean.makeabove_above': makeabove puts b above c when r a c
Arrow.lean.makeabove_below: makeabove puts c above b when ¬r a c
Arrow.lean.pivot_exists: Every alternative has a pivotal individual (step 2)
Arrow.lean.pivot_is_dictator_except_b: Pivot is dictator on pairs not involving b (step 3)
Arrow.lean.no_perfect_swf: No SWF satisfies all three desirable properties

Shapley.lean.shapley_null_player: Shapley value satisfies null player axiom
Shapley.lean.shapley_uniqueness: Uniqueness: Shapley value is the unique solution satisfying all four axioms
Shapley.lean.phi_unanimity: Any axiom-satisfying solution on unanimity game gives 1/|T| or 0
